contentBody: Which problem is best described as a regression task?
option.1: Predict whether an email is spam or not spam
option.2: Assign each customer to one of three loyalty tiers
option.3: Detect whether an image contains a cat or a dog
option.4: Predict the exact cab fare in rupees from distance and time of day
answerExplanation: Regression predicts a continuous numeric outcome. The other options are classification or labeling tasks.

contentBody: In linear regression the coefficient on a feature is best described as:
option.1: The learned multiplier showing how much the prediction changes per one-unit increase in that feature
option.2: The average of all target values in the training set
option.3: The number of rows in the training data
option.4: The random seed used for train_test_split
answerExplanation: The intercept is the baseline; coefficients are the per-feature slopes or weights.

contentBody: Select all statements that correctly use the vocabulary from this session.
option.1: Any column of integers such as job level coded 1 2 3 is always a valid regression target without further thought.
option.2: The target variable is the numeric outcome the model predicts.
option.3: Features are input columns used to form predictions.
option.4: Linear regression predicts the target as a weighted combination of features plus an intercept.
answerExplanation: Ordered category codes may be labels disguised as numbers; they are not necessarily a meaningful continuous scale.

contentBody: You have one feature: 200 study-hour values (one per student). For sklearn LinearRegression.fit with X and y how should X be organized?
option.1: A two-dimensional table with 1 row and 200 columns
option.2: A square table with 200 rows and 200 columns
option.3: A two-dimensional table with 200 rows and 1 column each row one student one feature
option.4: A single one-dimensional list of 200 numbers with only one axis
answerExplanation: scikit-learn expects X to be two-dimensional one row per sample one column per feature; with one feature that means 200 rows and 1 column.

contentBody: Which statements about train_test_split and generalisation are correct?
option.1: If test error is much worse than training error the model may have overfit the training data.
option.2: The test set should be used during fit so the model learns from harder examples.
option.3: The model should be fit only on the training portion not on the test portion.
option.4: Comparing training error to test error helps detect overfitting.
answerExplanation: The test set is held out for evaluation not for training.

contentBody: The residual for one data point is defined as:
option.1: The training loss summed over all points
option.2: The coefficient of the first feature
answerExplanation: Residual equals actual minus predicted; positive means the model under-predicted.

contentBody: Which patterns are consistent with the session discussion of residuals?
option.1: If residuals are mostly negative the model always has a bug in predict.
option.2: A strongly positive average residual suggests the model tends to under-predict on average.
option.3: Residuals scattered randomly around zero suggest no strong systematic bias in that respect.
option.4: A few very large residuals may indicate outliers or unusual cases worth inspecting.
answerExplanation: Systematic bias is a modeling or data issue not proof of a coding bug by itself.

option.1: Both training and test error are high because the model is too simple to capture the pattern
option.2: Training error is low but test error is much higher
option.3: The model memorises training labels perfectly and achieves 0 error on the test set
option.4: The average residual on training data is exactly zero so the model must be wrong
answerExplanation: Underfitting means the model is too simple with high error on both train and test. A large train-test gap is more typical of overfitting.
